examples.description: right ankle — full musculoskeletal/sports-medicine note: structured history and medications, observed gait and loading, labelled exam manoeuvres, imaging per view, and an assessment + plan synthesized and cited from the captured findings (Grounded Synthesis Mode, v3.2)

transcript.text: Right ankle pain. I rolled it playing soccer about three weeks ago and it hasn't settled.
I came down on it awkwardly after a jump, no contact, and the foot turned inwards. The pain is on the outside of the ankle.
It swelled up that night, and it still feels like it gives way on uneven ground. There's no locking.
I usually run about thirty kilometres a week and play soccer twice a week, but I've stopped both since the injury.
I've been resting it, icing, and using an elastic ankle brace, and I take naproxen 500 milligrams twice a day with food.
Let me watch you walk. There's an antalgic gait with reduced time on the right foot.
You're weight-bearing but you can't do a single-leg heel raise on the right, and a single-leg stance on the right is unsteady.
On inspection there's swelling over the lateral malleolus on the right with some bruising, no deformity.
There's tenderness on palpation over the anterior talofibular ligament on the right ankle.
Range of motion: dorsiflexion is reduced to about 10 degrees on the right compared with 20 on the left, plantarflexion is full.
The anterior drawer test is positive on the right and negative on the left, with an increased talar tilt on the right.
On the weight-bearing AP and mortise views of the right ankle the bony structures are intact with no fracture and a normal mortise.
On longitudinal ultrasound there's thickening and irregularity of the anterior talofibular ligament on the right with a small amount of surrounding fluid.
I'll order an MRI of the right ankle to look at the lateral ligaments and the cartilage.
I'll refer you to physiotherapy for a proprioception and peroneal strengthening program.
Keep using the brace for now, hold off on running and soccer, and we'll reassess in four weeks after the MRI.

claims.text: Right ankle pain following an inversion injury playing soccer approximately three weeks ago, not yet settled.
claims.source_quote: Right ankle pain. I rolled it playing soccer about three weeks ago and it hasn't settled.

claims.text: Non-contact inversion mechanism after landing from a jump, with pain at the lateral aspect of the right ankle.
claims.source_quote: I came down on it awkwardly after a jump, no contact, and the foot turned inwards. The pain is on the outside of the ankle.

claims.text: Immediate swelling on the night of injury, ongoing sensation of giving way on uneven ground, and no mechanical locking.
claims.source_quote: It swelled up that night, and it still feels like it gives way on uneven ground. There's no locking.

claims.text: Usual activity of running approximately 30 km per week and soccer twice weekly, both stopped since the injury.
claims.source_quote: I usually run about thirty kilometres a week and play soccer twice a week, but I've stopped both since the injury.

claims.text: Management tried to date: rest, icing, and an elastic ankle brace.
claims.source_quote: I've been resting it, icing, and using an elastic ankle brace, and I take naproxen 500 milligrams twice a day with food.

claims.text: Naproxen 500 mg orally twice daily with food.
claims.source_quote: I take naproxen 500 milligrams twice a day with food.

claims.text: Antalgic gait with reduced stance time on the right foot.
claims.source_quote: There's an antalgic gait with reduced time on the right foot.

claims.text: Weight-bearing but unable to perform a single-leg heel raise on the right, with an unsteady single-leg stance on the right.
claims.source_quote: You're weight-bearing but you can't do a single-leg heel raise on the right, and a single-leg stance on the right is unsteady.

claims.text: Inspection: swelling over the right lateral malleolus with bruising, no deformity.
claims.source_quote: On inspection there's swelling over the lateral malleolus on the right with some bruising, no deformity.

claims.text: Palpation: tenderness over the anterior talofibular ligament of the right ankle.
claims.source_quote: There's tenderness on palpation over the anterior talofibular ligament on the right ankle.

claims.text: Range of motion: right ankle dorsiflexion reduced to approximately 10 degrees versus 20 degrees on the left; plantarflexion full.
claims.source_quote: dorsiflexion is reduced to about 10 degrees on the right compared with 20 on the left, plantarflexion is full.

claims.text: Special tests: anterior drawer positive on the right and negative on the left, with increased talar tilt on the right.
claims.source_quote: The anterior drawer test is positive on the right and negative on the left, with an increased talar tilt on the right.

claims.text: Weight-bearing AP and mortise views of the right ankle: bony structures intact, no fracture, normal mortise.
claims.source_quote: On the weight-bearing AP and mortise views of the right ankle the bony structures are intact with no fracture and a normal mortise.

claims.text: Longitudinal ultrasound of the right ankle: thickening and irregularity of the anterior talofibular ligament with a small amount of surrounding fluid.
claims.source_quote: On longitudinal ultrasound there's thickening and irregularity of the anterior talofibular ligament on the right with a small amount of surrounding fluid.

claims.text: Working assessment: right anterior talofibular ligament injury with lateral ankle instability — positive anterior drawer and increased talar tilt on the right, anterior talofibular ligament tenderness, and ultrasound thickening of the same ligament.
claims.source_quote: The anterior drawer test is positive on the right and negative on the left, with an increased talar tilt on the right.

additional_sources.source_quote: There's tenderness on palpation over the anterior talofibular ligament on the right ankle.
On longitudinal ultrasound there's thickening and irregularity of the anterior talofibular ligament on the right with a small amount of surrounding fluid.

claims.text: Functional deficit on the right — antalgic gait, inability to perform a single-leg heel raise, and reduced dorsiflexion to approximately 10 degrees.
claims.source_quote: you can't do a single-leg heel raise on the right, and a single-leg stance on the right is unsteady.

additional_sources.source_quote: There's an antalgic gait with reduced time on the right foot.
dorsiflexion is reduced to about 10 degrees on the right compared with 20 on the left, plantarflexion is full.

claims.text: Investigations: MRI of the right ankle to assess the lateral ligaments and cartilage.
claims.source_quote: I'll order an MRI of the right ankle to look at the lateral ligaments and the cartilage.

claims.text: Referrals: physiotherapy for a proprioception and peroneal strengthening program.
claims.source_quote: I'll refer you to physiotherapy for a proprioception and peroneal strengthening program.

claims.text: Activity and follow-up: continue the brace, withhold running and soccer for now, and reassess in four weeks after the MRI.
claims.source_quote: Keep using the brace for now, hold off on running and soccer, and we'll reassess in four weeks after the MRI.
